description: World's largest manufacturer of MLCCs (Multilayer Ceramic Capacitors) and a leading supplier of passive electronic components, wireless modules, and RF filters. Headquartered in Nagaokakyo, Kyoto. Core products: MLCCs, SAW/BAW filters for smartphone RF front-ends, inductors, EMI filters, piezoelectric components, DC-DC converters, wireless connectivity modules (Bluetooth, Wi-Fi), and MEMS sensors. BAW (Bulk Acoustic Wave) filters are critical for 5G smartphone RF front-ends — produced at Kanazawa Murata Manufacturing Co., Ltd. (金沢村田製作所) which uses SAMCO compound semiconductor process equipment for filter fabrication. Murata is also a supplier of electronic components to SAMCO for inclusion in SAMCO's semiconductor process equipment — SAMCO holds a minor equity stake in Murata confirming the strategic bilateral relationship. TSE Prime Market. World market leader in MLCCs with ~40%+ global share.

majorSuppliers: SAMCO Inc. (6387.T, CMP-0060) — compound semiconductor process equipment for BAW filter manufacturing at Kanazawa Murata (confirmed in SAMCO securities filing)
Various ceramic raw material suppliers (barium titanate, rare earth materials)
Substrate and epitaxial wafer suppliers for SAW/BAW filter production
majorClients: Apple Inc. (AAPL) — largest customer, MLCCs and wireless modules for iPhone/iPad (~30%+ of Murata revenue)
Samsung Electronics — MLCCs, SAW/BAW filters, modules
Huawei — MLCCs and RF components (partially restricted by export controls)
Automotive OEMs and tier-1 suppliers — automotive-grade MLCCs
SAMCO Inc. (CMP-0060) — electronic components supplied to SAMCO for use in process equipment (bilateral relationship confirmed by SAMCO equity stake in Murata)
keyProducts: BAW (Bulk Acoustic Wave) filters — 5G RF front-ends
SAW (Surface Acoustic Wave) filters — smartphone RF
Chip inductors and EMI suppression filters
keySubsidiaries: Kanazawa Murata Manufacturing Co., Ltd. — BAW filter production (uses SAMCO equipment)
strategicHighlights: World's largest MLCC manufacturer (~40% global share)
Dominant in 5G BAW filter market with FBAR/BAW technology
Apple dependency (~30%+ revenue) creates both opportunity and risk
Heavy CAPEX investment in 5G filter and automotive-grade component production
Strategic equity investments in key technology partners (e.g., SAMCO holds minor stake in Murata)

dualRoleWithSAMCO.asClient: Kanazawa Murata Manufacturing uses SAMCO ICP-RIE/PECVD equipment for BAW filter (FBAR) fabrication
dualRoleWithSAMCO.asSupplier: Murata supplies electronic components used in SAMCO's semiconductor process equipment
dualRoleWithSAMCO.equityRelationship: SAMCO holds minor equity stake in Murata Manufacturing, cementing the strategic bilateral relationship

_externalRelationships.notes: Murata has a unique bilateral relationship with SAMCO: as a client (Kanazawa plant BAW filter production) and as a supplier (electronic components). Both confirmed by SAMCO's securities report and equity stake disclosure. Financial figures (market cap, PER, PBR) are estimates — requires dedicated research for accurate current data.

(subsidiary) is explicitly named as a SAMCO client in securities filing. SAMCO also holds equity stake in Murata. Financial data are estimates requiring dedicated research pass.
